source_milestone: R17 Agentic Operating Surface, A2A Runtime, and Kanban Release Cycle
purpose: Define the governed R17 board-event contract boundary without implementing executable handoffs, executable transitions, A2A dispatch, agent invocation, API calls, or board event replay.

required_policy_rules.statement: Events that move to closed require explicit user approval.
Developer must not approve evidence sufficiency.
Orchestrator must not bypass QA or audit gates.
Generated board contracts are design/contracts only, not runtime execution.
Board events may reference task packets, memory refs, evidence refs, and audit refs, but R17-004 must not implement those runtime flows.
Board state is a governed product surface, not a replacement for repo truth.
Fake multi-agent narration is not accepted as proof.
A2A runtime remains not implemented in R17-004.

required_non_claims: R17-004 defines governed card, board-state, and board-event contracts only
R17-004 does not implement board state store
R17-004 does not implement Kanban UI
R17-004 does not implement Orchestrator runtime
R17-004 does not implement A2A runtime
R17-004 does not implement Dev/Codex executor adapter
R17-004 does not implement QA/Test Agent adapter
R17-004 does not implement Evidence Auditor API adapter
R17-004 does not claim product runtime
R17-004 does not claim autonomous agents
R17-004 does not claim executable handoffs
R17-004 does not claim executable transitions
R17-004 does not claim external audit acceptance
R17-004 does not claim main merge
R13 boundary preserved
R14 caveats preserved
R15 caveats preserved
R16 boundary preserved
R17-005 through R17-028 remain planned only
R17-004 does not claim solved Codex compaction
R17-004 does not claim solved Codex reliability
